page: Microsoft Azure D16_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512F, and AVX512_VNNI, but lacks support for AVX512_BF16 and AMX extensions.

The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached volumes. Additionally, the instance does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the D16_v4 consistently in the average tier across multiple workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1269.0 and a multi-core score of 7938.0. In memory bandwidth tests, it records 830.75 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 82.97 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 45.16 GB/sec for large-block writes. For specialized tasks, it processes Redis SET operations at 13.89 million operations per second and delivers a static web serving throughput of 12.04 GB/sec.

A notable performance limitation is observed in large-scale language model inference; while the instance handles a small 135M model adequately, its prompt processing speed for a medium 7B model drops to a weak 22.86 tokens per second. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lack of local storage or GPU acceleration, the D16_v4 is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D16_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv4 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor operating at 2.6 GHz, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Benchmark results place the D16_v4 in the average performance tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7938.0 and a static web serving throughput of 12.04 GB/sec. While it handles small-scale LLM inference and general database operations efficiently, its performance drops to the bottom 25% when processing prompts for medium 7B models. This hardware profile makes the D16_v4 highly suitable for balanced workloads such as web serving, application hosting, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 64 GB RAM for reliable multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core).
No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage.
Delivers balanced mid-tier performance across CPU and memory benchmarks.
Exhibits weak performance on medium-sized LLM prompt processing.
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
